REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF CROSS-DEFTS MARY FIGONE AND NANCY STEWART'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES FILED BY PLAINTIFF FIGONE, MARY TRUSTEE OF THE FIGONE TRUST STUART, NANCY S.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS MARY FIGONE AND NANCY STEWART'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANTS ARE THE PREVAILING PARTIES ON THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO DISMISS THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT IS BASED ON CROSS-DEFENDANTS' PROTECTED FREE SPEECH OR PETITIONING ACTIVITY. CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO SHOW A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON HIS CLAIMS. THE VOLUNTARY DISMISSAL OF AN ALLEGED SLAPP SUIT WHILE A SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS PENDING CREATES A PRESUMPTION THAT CROSS-DEFENDANTS ARE PREVAILING PARTIES. CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO SHOW HE HAD DISMISSED BECAUSE HE HAD SUBSTANTIALLY ACHIEVED HIS GOAL THROUGH SETTLEMENT OR OTHER MEANS, OR FOR OTHER REASONS UNRELATED TO THE PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. (SEE COLTRAIN V. SHEWALTER (1998) 66 CAL.APP.4TH 94, 107, WHERE MONETARY LOSS WAS INSUFFICIENT TO DISPEL PRESUMPTION.) ATTORNEY FEES GRANTED. RATES REASONABLE AND TIME EXPENDED REASONABLE AND NECESSARY. ATTORNEY FEES AWARDED IN LODESTAR AMOUNT OF $15,640 PLUS $80 COSTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5759

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar May-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANTS MARY FIGONE AND NANCY STEWART'S Motion for Attorney's Fees. Appearances: James Brandan Kraus, Esq. of Zacks & Utrecht (415) 956-8100, appearing for Cross defendants and moving party; There are no other appearances. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS-DEFENDANTS ARE THE PREVAILING PARTIES ON THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO DISMISS THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT IS BASED ON CROSS-DEFENDANTS' PROTECTED FREE SPEECH OR PETITIONING ACTIVITY. CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO SHOW A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON HIS CLAIMS. THE VOLUNTARY DISMISSAL OF AN ALLEGED SLAPP SUIT WHILE A SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS PENDING CREATES A PRESUMPTION THAT CROSS-DEFENDANTS ARE PREVAILING PARTIES. CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO SHOW HE HAD DISMISSED BECAUSE HE HAD SUBSTANTIALLY ACHIEVED HIS GOAL THROUGH SETTLEMENT OR OTHER MEANS, OR FOR OTHER REASONS UNRELATED TO THE PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. (SEE COLTRAIN V. SHEWALTER (1998) 66 CAL.APP.4TH 94, 107, WHERE MONETARY LOSS WAS INSUFFICIENT TO DISPEL PRESUMPTION.) ATTORNEY FEES GRANTED. RATES REASONABLE AND TIME EXPENDED REASONABLE AND NECESSARY. ATTORNEY FEES AWARDED IN LODESTAR AMOUNT OF $15,640 PLUS $80 COSTS. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
